ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for passion fruit ginger soda; closest parent 'carbonated beverage' (FOODON_03301737) used as the ontology anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for passion fruit ginger soda; AN000A (soft drinks) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 'Carbonated soft drink' is the closest parent; no specific code exists for passion fruit ginger soda.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 ml (one glass), which is typical for a restaurant-served flavoured soda in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated beverage, fruit-flavoured, with added sugar — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 14148 and analogous fruit soda entries); supplemented with USDA FoodData Central entry for passion fruit juice (NDB No. 09315) for flavour-component reference
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for passion fruit ginger soda as a restaurant beverage. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drinks (approximately 8–10 g sugar per 100 ml, negligible protein and fat) combined with reference values for passion fruit juice (USDA NDB 09315) and ginger-flavoured sodas. The ginger component contributes negligible macronutrients at typical flavouring concentrations. Energy estimated at approximately 34 kcal per 100 ml based on sugar content. Sodium estimated at approximately 5 mg per 100 ml typical of lightly carbonated restaurant sodas.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for fruit juice and carbonated fruit drinks
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for passion fruit ginger soda. GI estimated at 52 based on the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drinks and passion fruit juice (GI approximately 46–58 range for fruit-based beverages with moderate sugar content). Ginger has a negligible GI contribution at flavouring concentrations. GI of 52 is the midpoint estimate, classified as low (GI < 55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 25.5 g carbohydrate in 300 ml serving) / 100 = 13.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (approximately 5 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Passion fruit ginger soda is very low in sodium (approximately 15 mg per 300 ml serving), making it acceptable for individuals managing hypertension, though the added sugar content warrants moderation to avoid excess caloric intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content contributing to glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 8 g of sugar per 100 ml and an estimated GI of 52, a standard 300 ml serving delivers approximately 24 g of sugar and a glycemic load of around 13, which individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for within their daily carbohydrate budget and monitor blood glucose response accordingly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; moderate added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Passion fruit ginger soda contains no fat, saturated fat, or cholesterol, which is favourable for cardiovascular health; however, regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased cardiovascular risk, so intake should be limited to occasional consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Passion Fruit Ginger Soda is a chilled, carbonated soft drink combining the tropical tartness of passion fruit with the warm, spicy notes of ginger, served over ice as a restaurant beverage. It is typically prepared by mixing passion fruit juice or pulp with ginger syrup or ginger extract and topping with carbonated water, resulting in a refreshing, lightly effervescent drink with a sweet-tart flavour profile and a mild ginger warmth on the finish. In Singapore, this style of house-made flavoured soda has become popular at casual dining restaurants, particularly those serving Chinese, dumpling, and noodle cuisines, as a non-alcoholic alternative to imported soft drinks. Nutritionally, the drink is essentially fat-free and protein-free, with its caloric content derived almost entirely from added sugars (approximately 8 g per 100 ml, 34 kcal per 100 ml). Passion fruit contributes small amounts of vitamin C and potassium. The estimated glycemic index of approximately 52 places it in the low GI category, though the glycemic load per standard 300 ml serving is moderate at approximately 13, which is clinically relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The ginger component may offer minor anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits at culinary concentrations, though these are not clinically significant at typical serving sizes. The beverage is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following halal or gluten-free diets.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for passion fruit ginger soda exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries for fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drinks and passion fruit juice. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for fruit-based carbonated beverages. Confidence is medium because the estimation basis is defensible and internally consistent, but exact restaurant-specific formulation data is unavailable.
notes: Protein_g is naturally zero as this is a sugar-sweetened carbonated beverage with no protein-contributing ingredients. Fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation — passion fruit juice, ginger syrup, and carbonated water contain no fat. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero consistent with a fat-free beverage. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this beverage category in the source database. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact codes exist for this specific beverage. All -1 sentinel values are documented above per the G3 sentinel values documentation rule.
